title: CoAP client uses predictable time-based Message ID
description:
File: rust/lichen-coap/src/client.rs:228-233

The mid_from_time() function generates Message IDs from the system clock subsecond nanoseconds:

    fn mid_from_time() -> u16 {
        use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
        SystemTime::now()
            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
            .map(|d| d.subsec_nanos() as u16)
            .unwrap_or(0x1234)
    }

This is predictable: an attacker who knows approximately when a request was sent can narrow down the MID to a small range, making spoofed response attacks easier.

Impact: Reduces security margin against response spoofing attacks. When combined with the fixed token and missing MID/token validation, forms a complete attack chain for response injection.

Additionally, if two requests are made within the same microsecond on fast systems, they will have the same MID, which violates the uniqueness requirement.

Fix: Use cryptographically random bytes or a monotonic counter for MID generation.
